images.caption: Figure 1:Diagram of the proposed hypernetwork-based approach for knowledge injection, showcasing a single example. Only hypernetwork parameters are learned whereas target model parameters are frozen.

a power-law fitâ„’val=1.02â‹…dâˆ’0.096\mathcal{L}_{\text{val}}=1.02\cdot d^{-0.096}.(b)All three OOD metrics improve with width, with fitted exponentsâˆ’0.100-0.100(OOD non-rephrased),âˆ’0.036-0.036(OOD rephrased), andâˆ’0.075-0.075(OOD MCQ).

Figure 3:Hypernetwork depth scaling results, showing final epoch loss vs. number of transformer layersLHNL_{\text{HN}}on a log-log scale.(a)Validation loss follows a power-law fitâ„’val=0.677â‹…Lâˆ’0.088\mathcal{L}_{\text{val}}=0.677\cdot L^{-0.088}.(b)All three
OOD metrics improve with depth, with fitted exponentsâˆ’0.096-0.096(OOD

Figure 4:Target model scaling results, showing final epoch loss vs. target
model parameter countTTon a log-log scale.(a)Validation loss
follows a power-law fitâ„’val=72â‹…Tâˆ’0.226\mathcal{L}_{\text{val}}=72\cdot T^{-0.226}.(b)All three OOD metrics improve with target model size, with
fitted exponentsâˆ’0.184-0.184(OOD non-rephrased),âˆ’0.107-0.107(OOD rephrased),

Figure 5:Fact count scaling results, showing final epoch loss vs. num.
of injected factsNNper example on a log-log scale.(a)Validation loss follows a power-lawâ„’val=0.667â‹…Nâˆ’0.080\mathcal{L}_{\text{val}}=0.667\cdot N^{-0.080}.(b)OOD

Figure 6:LoRA target model scaling results (r=16,Î±=32r=16,\alpha=32),

Figure 7:Full finetuning target model scaling results, showing final
epoch loss vs. target model parameter countTTon a log-log scale.(a)Validation loss follows a power-law fitâ„’val=90.6â‹…Tâˆ’0.249\mathcal{L}_{\text{val}}=90.6\cdot T^{-0.249}.(b)OOD
metrics improve with target model size, with fitted exponentsâˆ’0.183-0.183(OOD non-rephrased),âˆ’0.069-0.069(OOD rephrased), andâˆ’0.101-0.101(OOD MCQ). The 14B point was not evaluated due to compute constraints.

header: Appendix ACalculating Training Compute for Hypernetworks

images.caption:
Figure 8:Base model accuracy across all 39 knowledge domains.
The three held-out OOD domains (philosophy,linguistics,civil engineering) exhibit high base
model accuracy, lying approximately 4.8% above the mean of the
in-distribution domains.

Figure 9:Hyperparameter sweep results for the largest width configuration
(dmodel=1024d_{\text{model}}=1024), showing training loss on a log scale across
different learning rates, schedulers, and optimizers on a small-scale
subset. Cosine annealing with AdamW with learning rate5Ã—10âˆ’45\times 10^{-4}achieves the lowest final training loss, motivating its selection for all
scaling experiments.

Figure 10:LoRA rank scaling results, showing final epoch loss vs. LoRA
rankrr. Unlike other scaling axes, LoRA rank scaling exhibits a clear
saturating regime and is well-fit by a power-law with a constant offset:â„’â€‹(r)=aâ‹…rb+c\mathcal{L}(r)=a\cdot r^{b}+c. Fitted parameters:
validation0.102â‹…râˆ’0.913+0.5370.102\cdot r^{-0.913}+0.537, OOD non-rephrased0.117â‹…râˆ’0.854+0.5620.117\cdot r^{-0.854}+0.562, OOD rephrased0.0605â‹…râˆ’2.020+0.8000.0605\cdot r^{-2.020}+0.800, and OOD MCQ0.0932â‹…râˆ’0.903+0.7250.0932\cdot r^{-0.903}+0.725.

Figure 11:Loss trajectories for hypernetwork width scaling, showing
validation loss (left) and OOD non-rephrased loss (right) vs. cumulative
training FLOPs fordmodelâˆˆ{64,128,256,512,1024}d_{\text{model}}\in\{64,128,256,512,1024\}.

Figure 12:Loss trajectories for hypernetwork depth scaling, showing
validation loss (left) and OOD non-rephrased loss (right) vs. cumulative
training FLOPs forLHNâˆˆ{1,2,4,8,16}L_{\text{HN}}\in\{1,2,4,8,16\}. The narrow
FLOPs range reflects the near iso-compute nature of depth scaling
(see Table3).

Figure 13:Loss trajectories for fact count scaling, showing validation
loss (left) and OOD non-rephrased loss (right) vs. cumulative training
FLOPs forNfactsâˆˆ{2,4,8,16,32,52}N_{\text{facts}}\in\{2,4,8,16,32,52\}. Configurations
with more facts reach higher total FLOPs due to the largernhn_{h}(see Table4).

Figure 14:Loss trajectories for target model scaling, showing validation
loss (left) and OOD non-rephrased loss (right) vs. cumulative training
FLOPs for target model sizes 0.5B, 1.5B, 3B, 7B, and 14B. Larger target
models incur higher per-example FLOPs and therefore reach higher total
FLOPs at the same number of epochs.
